title: Tea and Consent
description: This classic animated video explains sexual consent simply and clearly using the analogy of making someone a cup of tea. Widely used in UK schools and police training.

title: What is Coercive Control?
description: Women's Aid explains what coercive control looks like in a relationship, how it escalates, and why it can be so hard to recognise from the inside.

title: Reporting Sexual Violence to the Police
description: Rape Crisis England & Wales walks through what the reporting process looks like, what to expect at each stage, and your rights as a victim.

title: What is an ISVA?
description: Explains the role of an Independent Sexual Violence Adviser — how they support you through the police investigation and court process, at no cost to you.

title: Non-Molestation Orders Explained
description: A solicitor explains what a non-molestation order is, how to apply, how quickly it can be granted, and what happens if it is breached.

title: Revenge Porn: Know Your Rights
description: Overview of the law around non-consensual intimate image sharing, what you can do immediately, and where to get specialist help.

title: Clare's Law — What Is It and How Do You Use It?
description: Explains the Domestic Violence Disclosure Scheme — your right to ask police if a partner has a history of abuse.

title: Understanding the Freeze Response in Trauma
description: A psychologist explains why people freeze during traumatic events — and why freezing is not consent. Important for understanding your own reaction.
